description_long: This object is a vintage wooden control cabinet with a rectangular form and two front doors that open outward, revealing a control panel with dials, switches, and fabric-covered speaker or ventilation panels. The exterior is finished in a warm, polished wood veneer, giving it a classic, mid-20th-century appearance. The interior houses a set of electronic components, including vacuum tubes and wiring, visible from the rear. The cabinet sits directly on the floor, with a solid base and no visible legs or mounting brackets. The doors feature simple, integrated pull handles without any visible locking mechanism. The overall design suggests it was intended for indoor use, possibly in a utility or technical room.
description: A vintage wooden control cabinet with double front doors, a polished finish, and an internal panel of dials and electronics.
description_view_0: Front view showing both doors open, revealing the control panel with dials, switches, and fabric panels.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the smooth, polished wooden exterior and the open door extending outward.
description_view_2: Rear view exposing the internal electronic components, including vacuum tubes and wiring.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the other door open, showing the continuous wood finish and door construction.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
